Our Mission

Silent Honor Foundation exists to give military veterans the financial education and support they didn't get when they left service. No cost. No catch. Just real help from people who've been there.

Silent Honor Foundation Inc. is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit. Contributions are tax-deductible.

Our Story

About Silent Honor
Foundation

Built by veterans who lived through financial hardship after service, Silent Honor exists because too many of our own fell through the cracks — and we refused to let that keep happening.

Born From Experience

We've Been
Where You Are

Silent Honor Foundation was started by veterans who dealt with the same garbage after getting out — wrecked credit, predatory lenders, and zero clue how the civilian financial world worked.

We got tired of watching fellow vets struggle with the same problems nobody prepared us for. So in 2023, we stopped complaining about the gap and started doing something about it. Silent Honor exists because we believe veterans deserve real financial education — not a pamphlet at TAPS.

We're not the VA. We're not a bank trying to sell you something. We're veterans who got certified, built the resources, and created the organization we wished existed when we needed it.

Certified Financial Education Instructor (CFEI)
Certified Credit Counselor
501(c)(3) Nonprofit · EIN 99-3172064
Serving veterans from all branches
Based in Missouri · Founded 2023
What Drives Us

Our Core Values

🎖️
Honor the Service

Every veteran earned something better than financial hardship. We exist to make sure that debt and poor credit don't define what comes after uniform.

📚
Education is the Mission

Knowledge is the most powerful financial tool that exists. We give it away for free because that's how you actually close the gap.

🤝
No Judgment, Ever

Financial hardship is not a character flaw. Veterans come to us from every starting point. Every single one gets the same level of respect and support.

🔒
Earned Trust

We are fiduciaries of the veterans we serve and the donors who fund us. We operate with full transparency, verified credentials, and documented results.

Leadership

Board of Directors

Silent Honor is governed by a board of directors committed to the financial empowerment of every veteran and military family we serve.

Michael Lugenbell
Co-Founder & Executive Director

U.S. Military and law enforcement veteran with over seven years of experience in credit repair and financial coaching. Co-founded Silent Honor Foundation to deliver the financial education system he wished had existed when he separated from service. Holds CFEI and Certified Credit Counselor credentials; actively pursuing the Accredited Financial Counselor (AFC) designation.

CFEI Credit Counselor Co-Founder Veteran
⭐
Ryan Hammer
Chairman of the Board

Chairman of the board and co-founding member of Silent Honor Foundation. U.S. Military veteran who brings a commitment to veteran advocacy and organizational governance that helps keep the mission grounded and accountable.

Co-Founder Chairman Veteran
⭐
Ryan Hemhouser
Board Director

Board director of Silent Honor Foundation. U.S. Military veteran committed to ensuring the organization operates with integrity, accountability, and a relentless focus on veteran outcomes.

What We Offer

Services & the
Veteran Network

Every service Silent Honor provides is free to eligible veterans and military families — plus access to a national network of veteran-owned businesses and member-only protection built for the industries veterans run.

Tell us where you're at — we'll lead with what matters most
Free for All Veterans

Financial Services

No hidden fees, no upsells, no strings attached. Every service below is 100% free to eligible veterans.

Service 01
💳
Available Now

Credit Review & Education

Your credit report is often the biggest obstacle between where you are and where you're trying to go. We pull it apart, explain what's there, and give you an actionable plan.

  • Full tri-bureau credit report review
  • Score factor analysis — what's hurting you and by how much
  • Dispute identification and guidance
  • Custom credit improvement roadmap
  • Military-specific credit protections (SCRA, MLA) explained
Get Started →
Service 02
📚
Available Now

Financial Literacy Training

Self-paced courses built specifically for veterans — not generic financial advice recycled from civilian playbooks. Real content for real situations veterans face.

  • Credit education course (7 lessons, fully live)
  • Financial literacy foundations course
  • Money Mission comprehensive course (in development)
  • All courses 100% free, self-paced, no registration required
  • Content designed by a CFEI with veteran-specific framing
View Courses →
Service 03
🎯
Available Now

One-on-One Financial Coaching

Personalized coaching sessions with a Certified Financial Education Instructor and Certified Credit Counselor. No judgment. No scripts. Just real, actionable guidance for your situation.

  • Budget creation and debt review
  • Debt payoff strategy (snowball, avalanche, hybrid)
  • Credit rebuilding plan with milestone tracking
  • Financial goal setting and 90-day action planning
  • Ongoing accountability check-ins
Get Matched →
Service 04
🏠
Coming Soon

VA Loan Closing Cost Assistance

A VA loan eliminates the down payment — but closing costs can still run $5,000–$15,000+. We're building a program to provide direct assistance to veterans who qualify, bridging that final gap to homeownership.

  • Direct closing cost assistance for qualifying veterans
  • VA loan eligibility review
  • Homeownership readiness assessment
  • Lender referrals with veteran-friendly track records
  • Pre-purchase financial coaching
Get Notified →
Service 05
📊
Available Now

Debt Management Planning

We help you build a realistic, strategic debt payoff plan based on your actual numbers — not a one-size-fits-all template.

  • Full debt inventory review
  • Debt-to-income ratio analysis
  • Snowball vs. avalanche comparison for your specific debts
  • Consolidation and refinance evaluation
  • Creditor hardship program identification
Get Started →
Service 06
🛡️
Coming Soon

VA Disability Support

Getting the VA disability rating you've earned is a fight — and most veterans don't know their full options. We're building a dedicated support service to help with documentation, appeals, and benefits maximization — no claim sharks taking a cut of your back pay.

  • Disability claim documentation guidance
  • Appeal support and strategy
  • Benefits maximization review
  • Referrals to accredited VA claims agents
Get Notified →

How It Works

Getting Started

Three steps to unlock every service, the veteran business network, and member protection.

1
Join as a Member

Fill out the free membership application. Takes about 5 minutes. We'll review and get back to you.

2
Apply for What You Need

Once approved, access coaching, courses, the business network, and protection through your member dashboard.

3
Complete Intake

We'll schedule an intake call to understand your situation and match you with the right resources.

Become a Member — It's Free →
Who Qualifies

Who We Serve

If you served in the U.S. military, you qualify. Silent Honor is exclusively for veterans — all branches, all eras.

Join Free →

Credit Education for Veterans

Reading Your Credit Report

Money Mission: Complete Financial Literacy

The comprehensive course Silent Honor is building — 9 modules, 34 lessons, and approximately 170 micro-segments covering everything a veteran needs to know about personal finance. From mindset and budgeting through investing and wealth building. This is the course that doesn't exist anywhere else for the military community.

Financial Foundations & Mindset
Budgeting (4 lessons)

Money Mission Preview

What's Inside the
Complete Course

Nine full modules covering every area of personal finance a veteran needs to master — built with CFEI frameworks and veteran-specific context throughout.

Module 01
Financial Foundations

3 lessons · Money mindset, your money story, the full financial picture — net worth, income, benefits

Module 02
Budgeting

4 lessons · What a budget is, methods (50/30/20, zero-based, envelope), building from scratch, making it stick

Module 03
Understanding Debt

4 lessons · How debt works, the true cost of debt, debt inventory, military-specific debt situations (SCRA, MLA, predatory lenders)

Module 04
Debt Payoff Strategies

4 lessons · Snowball method, avalanche method, side-by-side comparison, negotiating and managing debt

Module 05
Credit — Full Depth

7 lessons · Credit basics, score calculation, reading reports, building credit, rebuilding credit, disputing errors, identity protection

Module 06
Savings & Emergency Funds

3 lessons · Why savings comes first, building your emergency fund, savings goals beyond the emergency fund

Module 07
Income & Benefits

3 lessons · Your full income picture (VA disability, GI Bill, SSDI), employer benefits you're leaving behind, increasing your income

Module 08
Investing & Wealth Building

3 lessons · Investing basics, retirement accounts (TSP, BRS, IRA, 401k), building long-term wealth and breaking the cycle

Module 09
Your Financial Plan

3 lessons · Building your personal plan, handling setbacks, resources and next steps — what comes after the course

Reach Out

Contact Silent Honor

There's no wrong reason to reach out. Whether you need help with credit, a budget, a VA loan question, or just don't know where to start — we're here.

Response Time
We aim to respond to all inquiries within 2–3 business days. If your situation is urgent, please note that in your message.
Confidentiality
Everything you share with Silent Honor stays with Silent Honor. We do not sell, share, or disclose your information. Our coaches operate under professional ethical standards.
Common Questions

FAQ

Is everything really free? ↓
Yes. Every course, every coaching session, every credit review — 100% free to eligible veterans and military families. Silent Honor is funded by donors, not the veterans we serve. There are no hidden fees, no upsells, and no subscription required.
Do I need a good credit score to work with you? ↓
Absolutely not. Most of the people we serve have credit challenges — that's exactly why we exist. We work with veterans at every starting point. The lower your score, the more we can help. Zero judgment, ever.
What branches of service do you serve? ↓
All of them. Army, Navy, Air Force, Marine Corps, Coast Guard, Space Force, National Guard, and Reserve — plus surviving spouses and immediate family members. Every era of service is welcome.
Are you a credit repair company? ↓
No. We are a nonprofit financial education and coaching organization. We don't charge for credit services and we don't make promises we can't keep. We teach veterans how to understand and improve their own credit, and we coach them through the process — but we are educators and counselors, not a credit repair service.
Who does the coaching? ↓
Coaching is led by Michael Lugenbell, a Certified Financial Education Instructor (CFEI) and Certified Credit Counselor with 7+ years of experience working with veterans and families. As we scale, all coaches added to the team will hold equivalent professional credentials.
Can I donate to support the mission? ↓
Yes — and we're deeply grateful for it. Donations are what make free programs possible. Silent Honor Foundation is a registered 501(c)(3) and all contributions are tax-deductible. Visit our donate page or reach out directly.

Empowering veterans with the financial education, credit counseling, and tools needed to build strong, self-sufficient futures.
